Predicate not defined in domain:
  and
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: examine a trophy with a lamp
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images depict a simple indoor scene with a brown, textured wall and floor. The domain file defines specific object types (agent, microwave, fridge, sink, knife, receptacle, object) and predicates for planning, but the visual content does not contain any of these domain-specific objects.

The scene includes:
- A dark brown table with four angled legs and a single drawer with a gold-colored knob.
- A small, dark metallic statue of a human figure in a dynamic pose, placed on a square, light-gray base, which sits atop the table.
- A portion of a wooden structure, possibly a chair or shelf, visible on the right side of the image, with a light brown leg and a gray base plate.
- A reddish-brown object, possibly a book or box, partially visible on the left side of the table.

None of the objects in the scene match the types defined in the domain (microwave, fridge, sink, knife, or receptacle). The statue and table are generic objects not classified under the domain's types. The instruction to "examine a trophy with a lamp" cannot be fulfilled as there is no trophy or lamp visible in the scene. The domain's predicates (e.g., opened, inReceptacle, holds, isClean, etc.) are not applicable because the required objects and their states are not present.

In summary, the scene lacks any objects relevant to the domain or the instruction, and no spatial relationships or states defined in the domain can be described. The visual elements are purely decorative and do not support the planning task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

The provided scene does not contain any objects that match the types defined in the domain file (microwave, fridge, sink, knife, or receptacle). The instruction to "examine a trophy with a lamp" cannot be fulfilled as there is no trophy or lamp visible in the scene. The domain's predicates (e.g., opened, inReceptacle, holds, isClean, etc.) are not applicable because the required objects and their states are not present.

Therefore, it is not possible to generate a valid PDDL problem file that adheres to the domain constraints and accurately reflects the scene. The scene lacks the necessary objects and relationships defined in the domain.

However, to comply with the output format requirement, here is a minimal PDDL problem file that declares the agent and an object (the statue, treated as a generic object), with no applicable actions or goals, since none of the domain actions can be performed:

```pddl
(define (problem examine_trophy_with_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        statue - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 statue)
    )
    (:goal (and))
)
```

Note: This problem is trivial and does not fulfill the instruction, as the domain and scene are incompatible. No meaningful planning can be performed.
attempts.problem:
(define (problem examine_trophy_with_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        statue - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 statue)
    )
    (:goal (and))
)

    Instruction: examine a trophy with a lamp
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images depict a scene with several objects, but none of them match the types defined in the given PDDL domain file (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`, or `agent`). The domain is focused on kitchen-related tasks involving heating, cooling, cleaning, slicing, and placing objects in receptacles. The instruction "examine a trophy with a lamp" also does not align with the domain's predicates or actions.

Here is a detailed description of the visible objects in the images, noting their incompatibility with the domain:

1.  **Objects and their types (Domain Relevance):**
    *   **Trophy:** A small, dark, glossy statuette of a human figure is visible on a black table. It is mounted on a dark rectangular base. This object is not defined in the domain.
    *   **Lamp:** A lamp with a brownish-orange, possibly wooden, base and a dark, angled lampshade is visible on the right side of the scene. The lamp is on a black table. This object is not defined in the domain.
    *   **Table:** A black, rectangular table with thin, angled legs is present. It holds the trophy and the lamp. This object is not defined as a `receptacle` in the domain, although it functions as a surface. The domain does not have a generic "table" type.
    *   **Floor and Walls:** The scene has a brown, textured floor and plain brown walls. These are not objects in the domain.
    *   **Grid Panel:** A brown, gridded panel is visible on the right wall. This is not an object in the domain.

2.  **Quantities of each object type:**
    *   1 Trophy
    *   1 Lamp
    *   1 Table
    *   1 Grid Panel
    *   0 Objects of type `microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, or `agent`.

3.  **Spatial Relationships (Domain Predicate Relevance):**
    *   The trophy is `on` the table. (The domain has no `isOn` predicate for objects on surfaces, only for toggled objects or objects in receptacles).
    *   The lamp is `on` the table. (Same as above).
    *   The table is `on` the floor.
    *   The grid panel is `on` the wall.
    *   No objects are `inReceptacle` as there are no defined receptacles.
    *   No agent is present, so `holds`, `holdsAny`, `atLocation` for an agent are all false or undefined.
    *   No objects are `opened`, `isClean`, `isHot`, `isCool`, `isSliced`, or `isToggled` as per the domain's predicates, as these states are not applicable to the visible objects or the scene context.

4.  **Current State of each object relevant to the planning task:**
    *   The trophy is stationary on the table.
    *   The lamp is stationary on the table. Its state (on/off) is not visually discernible, and the domain's `isOn` predicate does not apply to lamps in this context.
    *   The table is stationary.
    *   No actions from the domain (GotoLocation, OpenObject, PickupObject, etc.) can be performed as the required objects and agent are not present or defined.

5.  **Colors, positions, and other visual properties:**
    *   **Trophy:** Dark, almost black, glossy finish. Positioned on the left side of the black table.
    *   **Lamp:** Base is brownish-orange, lampshade is dark. Positioned on the right side of the black table.
    *   **Table:** Black, rectangular, with thin, angled legs. Positioned in the center of the visible floor area.
    *   **Floor:** Brown, textured, possibly carpeted.
    *   **Walls:** Plain, solid brown.
    *   **Grid Panel:** Brown, with a visible grid pattern, mounted vertically on the right wall.

In summary, the scene contains a trophy and a lamp on a table, but these objects and the overall environment do not conform to the PDDL domain `put_task`. The domain's predicates and actions are inapplicable to this scene. The instruction to "examine a trophy with a lamp" cannot be translated into a sequence of actions within the given domain.

Since no objects in the scene match the types defined in the domain (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, `receptacle`, or `agent`), and no domain-defined predicates or actions can be applied to the scene or the instruction, it is impossible to generate a valid PDDL problem that adheres to the domain constraints.

Therefore, the correct response is that **no valid PDDL problem can be generated** for this scene and instruction under the given domain.

No valid PDDL problem can be generated because the scene and instruction are incompatible with the provided domain. The domain does not define the objects present (trophy, lamp, table) or the action "examine", and no domain-relevant objects or relationships exist in the scene.

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
